bids.reasoning: The opponent's opening offer gives me the absolute lowest possible utility (valueE on every issue) despite claiming it is a "win-win." Since we are at the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0), I will counter by proposing my maximum utility values across all issues. This establishes a strong baseline for my side.

The opponent has repeated their initial lowest-utility offer while claiming it's a "win-win." Since it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02), I will ignore their rhetoric and provide a slight concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I will maintain my highest-value preferences on the most important issues (A, B, C) while making a minor concession to `valueB` on the lower-weight issues (D, E) to keep my overall utility at 0.96.

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.04), so there's no need to make large concessions. The opponent claims to be offering a "win-win" but their proposals give me near-minimal utility, though they did concede slightly on issueA. I will maintain my top values on my highest-priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while making incremental concessions on my lowest-priority issues (issueD and issueE) to show flexibility and probe the opponent's preferences.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent has offered extremely low utility to me so far, conceding minimally on issueA while keeping all other issues at their lowest values. I will continue to hold firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB), while making a measured concession on issueC to signal willingness to negotiate and encourage them to concede further.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.08), and the opponent has made only very minor concessions on my most important issues, asking almost exclusively for my lowest utility values. To show a willingness to compromise while preserving my core interests, I will concede slightly more on my least important issues (issueD and issueE) while remaining firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB). This offer still yields a high utility of 0.84 for me.

We are very early in the negotiation, and I can see from the offer history that you have made small concessions on `issueA` and `issueB`. Since these happen to be my most heavily weighted issues, I will hold firm on them. In exchange, I am offering a concession on `issueD` and `issueE`, which seem to be highly important to you, to work toward a mutually beneficial agreement.

The opponent claims to have made "significant concessions" on issueA and issueB, but their offers of valueD are still very low utility for me. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), I will hold firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB) while fully conceding on my least important issue (issueD) to match their demand for valueE, and making a slight concession on issueC. This keeps my utility above 0.80 while showing a willingness to compromise on issues that matter less to me.

The current time is very early (t=0.14). The opponent has been testing tradeoffs between issueA and issueB, making small concessions on one while retracting them on the other. Because issueA and issueB are my highest-weight issues (0.3 each), I will hold firm on them. I will offer a further concession on my lowest-utility issues, issuing valueE for both issueD and issueE, while maintaining a high utility (0.80) to guide the opponent toward mutually beneficial tradeoffs.

The opponent has made a major concession by offering my most preferred value for issueA (valueA), which carries high weight for me. I will eagerly lock in issueA at valueA. In exchange, to reciprocate and keep the negotiation progressing, I will concede on issueC down to valueC, while keeping issueD and issueE at valueE (the opponent's consistent preference). I will hold firm on issueB at valueA for now, as it is also highly weighted for me and early in the negotiation (t=0.16).

The negotiation is still early (t=0.18). The opponent has shown a willingness to concede significantly on issueA (up to valueA) and moderately on issueB and issueC in different offers, while rigidly holding issueD and issueE. Since my weights for issueD and issueE are the lowest (0.1 each), I will continue to concede on those to accommodate their likely preferences. I will target a utility around 0.78 by holding firm on issueA and issueC, while introducing a slight concession on issueB to encourage further compromise.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.2), so I will aim for a utility around 0.78. I notice you proposed `valueB` for `issueA` in your last offer, so I am willing to accept that concession to show flexibility. In exchange, I need you to accommodate my high-priority needs on `issueB` and `issueC`, while I continue to concede significantly on your likely highest-priority issues `issueD` and `issueE`.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.22), so my priority is to maintain a high utility (around 0.80) while testing varying combinations to map opponent preferences. The opponent's recent offers show some flexibility on issueA and issueB but much less on issueC and issueD, whereas they just showed a slight concession on issueE. Therefore, I will claim high value on my most important issues (issueA and issueB, as well as issueC) while conceding completely on my lowest priority issue (issueD) and meeting them at their slightly conceded value on issueE.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.24), and I see you are rotating concessions across issues A, B, and C, having recently offered valueC for issueB and valueD for issueC. I am willing to concede my lower-weight issues (D and E) to your strongly preferred values while maintaining high utility for myself on issues A and B, which seem to be acceptable given your t=0.16 offer of valueA. This ensures I maintain an overall utility above 0.8.

The opponent has made a significant concession by offering `valueA` on `issueA`, which is one of my highest-weighted issues. Since we are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will lock in this mutual gain on `issueA` while slowly conceding on the lower-weighted issues (`issueD` and `issueE`). I will maintain high utility expectations on `issueB` and `issueC` for now, yielding slightly on `issueD` and `issueE` to encourage further movement.

The opponent has shown a willingness to concede on issueA (having offered valueA) and just recently offered valueC on issueD. Since we are still in the early-to-mid stages (t=0.28), I want to maintain a high utility (around 0.80) while acknowledging their concessions. I will lock in issueA at valueA, keep issueB at valueA, give ground on issueC, match their concession on issueD to valueC, and fully concede issueE to valueE, which they continually demand.

The current time fraction is 0.3, meaning it is still relatively early in the negotiation. The opponent has tried a variety of offers and their messages are making typical "win-win" claims, all while trying to give me their preferred "valueE" on most issues, though they recently offered "valueC" on issueB. I will continue to hold firm on my highest-priority issues, issueA and issueB, while making further measured concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueD and issueE) to pull the opponent toward a deal.

The opponent has made significant concessions, particularly on issueA (finally offering valueA). However, the total utility of their last offer remains below 0.60 for me, as they ask for heavy compromises on issues B and C. At time `t = 0.32`, I can reward their move on issueA by making a gentle concession on issueB and issueD, aiming for a counter-offer that balances near a 0.75 utility threshold. I am continuing to stand firm where my issues are most heavily weighted while trying to meet them on some lower-weight issues like issueE.

I am targeting an overall utility around 0.75 as we are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.34). The opponent has shown willingness to concede fully on issueA (valueA) and partially on issueC (valueC), while remaining stubborn on issueD and issueE. I will fully accept their demands on my lowest-weight issues (issueD and issueE to valueE), lock in the agreed issueA to valueA, and ask for moderate favorable outcomes on issueB and issueC.

The opponent is showing flexibility by alternating concessions between issueA and issueB, recently offering valueA for issueA and valueA for issueB in separate bids. I will maintain high demands on issueA and issueB (my highest weights) by proposing valueA for both, while making a partial concession to valueB on issueC and full concessions on the less important issueD and issueE. This allows me to target a strong 0.80 utility while providing significant compromises on their prioritized issues.

The opponent's t=0.36 proposal included `issueA: valueB` and `issueB: valueA`, indicating their willingness to afford me high value on my most critical issues. I am adopting these exact values for issues A and B to lock in 0.54 utility. To make this attractive and reciprocate their concessions, I am fully conceding issues D and E to `valueE`, and meeting them partially in the middle on issueC with `valueC`, targeting a solid mid-game utility of 0.70.

The time fraction is 0.40, so we have plenty of time. The opponent has started offering me `valueA` on either `issueA` or `issueB`, alternating while demanding large concessions elsewhere. To move towards an agreement, I will continue conceding on my lowest importance issues (`issueD` and `issueE`), adopting `valueE` for both which they frequently propose. Conversely, I will continue to hold firm on my heavily weighted issues, proposing `valueB` for `issueA` and `valueA` for `issueB` to maintain a utility of around 0.74.

The opponent significantly conceded on `issueA` to valueA and `issueC` to valueB in their last offer, but they are holding firmly to valueE on `issueB`, `issueD`, and `issueE`. Since the deadline is approaching the midpoint (t=0.42), I will secure their concession on `issueA` while making a compromise on `issueB` (lowering it to valueC) and holding firm on `issueC` near the top. This results in an offer with a strong utility of 0.72 for me, while responding to their insistence on modifying `issueB`.

The opponent is consistently outputting repetitive messages asserting fairness while their offers hover around 0.45-0.54 utility for me. Because we are still mid-game (t=0.44), I will systematically yield ground on the lower-weighted issues (issueD and issueE) while holding relatively firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC). This allows me to test if they are willing to accept a compromise where they get their preferred values on the minor issues while giving me strong utility on the major ones.

The opponent's messages consist of repetitive, boilerplate language claiming to offer a "win-win" balance, but their actual bids show they are willing to concede on issueD (valueC at t=0.46) and issueE (valueD at t=0.46). Since we are at t=0.46, I will target an overall utility of approximately 0.80 by maintaining strong positions on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB) while incorporating the opponent's recent concessions on issueD and issueE. This allows me to propose a collaborative combination without sacrificing much value.

The current time fraction is 0.48, placing us close to the midpoint of the negotiation. I am targeting a utility of approximately 0.78, holding relatively firm on my highest-priority issues, issueA and issueB, where the opponent has already shown some willingness to concede on separate occasions (e.g., offering `valueA` for issueA at t=0.44 and for issueB at t=0.40). The opponent's messages emphasize they are making "meaningful concessions," but since this is cheap talk, I rely solely on their bid history to inform my strategic tradeoffs while offering slightly more favorable terms on the lower-weight issues (issueD and issueE) to find an agreeable middle ground.

The opponent has repeatedly signaled strong preference for issues D and E (mostly bidding valueE) while toggling their concessions between issueA (offering valueA at t=0.42) and issueB (offering valueA at t=0.50). This suggests they view A and B as tradeable. At t=0.50, I will seek a utility of ~0.72 by acknowledging their flexibility on A and B, maintaining a strong claim on issueC, and fully concedeing on their most prioritized issues D and E.

The time fraction is just over half at 0.52. The opponent's recent offer included valueD for issueC and valueB for issueE, suggesting these are valuable concessions for them. Recognizing this, I will incorporate these specific values for the lower-weight issues (issueC, issueD, issueE) while keeping my highest priority issues (issueA and issueB) at my maximum utility (valueA). This allows me to test if they will accept my top values on major issues when I concede perfectly on their recent minor-issue demands, targeting a solid ~0.78 utility.

We have passed the halfway point (t=0.54), so it is time to start making more targeted concessions while protecting my highest weight issues (issueA and issueB). The opponent consistently asks for 'valueE' on issueD and issueE, so I will fully concede on those low-weight issues to find common ground. I will ask for 'valueA' on issueA and 'valueB' for issueB and issueC in return, targeting an overall utility around 0.74.
